Default JerryScript engine port API functions for setting and getting log levels and abort behavior.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the default port API for the JerryScript engine. It provides function declarations for managing the behavior of the engine's logging and error handling. The functions [`jerry_port_default_set_abort_on_fail`](<#jerry_port_default_set_abort_on_fail>) and [`jerry_port_default_is_abort_on_fail`](<#jerry_port_default_is_abort_on_fail>) allow the user to configure and check if the engine should abort on failure. Additionally, [`jerry_port_default_get_log_level`](<#jerry_port_default_get_log_level>) and [`jerry_port_default_set_log_level`](<#jerry_port_default_set_log_level>) are used to retrieve and set the logging level of the engine. The header file ensures compatibility with C++ by using `extern "C"` to prevent name mangling when included in C++ projects.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `jerry-port.h`
- `stdbool.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### jerry\_port\_default\_set\_abort\_on\_fail<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jerry_port_default_set_abort_on_fail}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/default/jerry-port-default.h#L34>)

Sets the 'abort on fail' flag to the specified value.
- **Description**: Use this function to control whether the system should abort on failure. This function is part of the default Jerry engine port API and is available only when the default port is used. It is important to set this flag according to the desired behavior of the system in case of failure. Ensure that the system is in a state where changing this behavior is safe and will not disrupt ongoing operations.
- **Inputs**:
    - `flag`: A boolean value that determines the new state of the 'abort on fail' flag. If true, the system will abort on failure; if false, it will not. The caller must provide a valid boolean value.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jerry_port_default_set_abort_on_fail`](<jerry-port-default-fatal.c.md#jerry_port_default_set_abort_on_fail>)  (Implementation)


---
### jerry\_port\_default\_is\_abort\_on\_fail<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jerry_port_default_is_abort_on_fail}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/default/jerry-port-default.h#L35>)

Checks if the abort-on-fail mode is enabled.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the system is configured to abort on failure. This function is part of the default Jerry engine port API and is available only when the default port is used. It is useful for checking the current configuration state related to error handling.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a boolean value: `true` if abort-on-fail mode is enabled, `false` otherwise.
- **See Also**: [`jerry_port_default_is_abort_on_fail`](<jerry-port-default-fatal.c.md#jerry_port_default_is_abort_on_fail>)  (Implementation)

### jerry\_port\_default\_get\_log\_level<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jerry_port_default_get_log_level}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/default/jerry-port-default.h#L37>)

Retrieves the current log level for the Jerry engine.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current log level setting of the Jerry engine when the default port is in use. This function is useful for checking the verbosity of log messages that the engine will produce. It does not require any parameters and can be called at any time to get the current log level.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns the current log level as a `jerry_log_level_t` value.
- **See Also**: [`jerry_port_default_get_log_level`](<jerry-port-default-io.c.md#jerry_port_default_get_log_level>)  (Implementation)


---
### jerry\_port\_default\_set\_log\_level<!-- {{#callable_declaration:jerry_port_default_set_log_level}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/targets/default/jerry-port-default.h#L38>)

Sets the logging level for the Jerry engine.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify the desired logging level for the Jerry engine. This function must be called to adjust the verbosity of log messages according to the specified level. It is important to ensure that the `level` parameter is a valid `jerry_log_level_t` value to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `level`: Specifies the logging level to set. Must be a valid `jerry_log_level_t` value. The caller must ensure the value is within the defined range of logging levels.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`jerry_port_default_set_log_level`](<jerry-port-default-io.c.md#jerry_port_default_set_log_level>)  (Implementation)
